buckets.name: site-content
buckets.notes: Migrating AWS S3 bucket for site content. Using sjc (US West) as default region.

easy_wins: Railway Buckets provide S3-compatible storage with simpler configuration
No need for complex bucket policies - Railway handles authentication
limitations: Railway Buckets don't support bucket policies - access control is simplified
Railway doesn't have Route53 equivalent - DNS must be managed elsewhere
Limited bucket regions compared to AWS (only sjc, iad, ams, sin available)
manual_steps: Create bucket using: railway bucket create site-content --region sjc
Get credentials: railway bucket credentials --bucket site-content --json
Update DNS settings in your domain provider instead of Route53
Generate a new random string for password if needed (Railway has built-in secret management)

lambda_analysis.reason: No Lambda functions to migrate in this configuration
